name: Cuban Black Bean Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort and soak beans overnight; drain and discard the soak water.
2. In a 4-quart pot, sauté onion in butter or margarine until tender but not browned.
3. Add soaked beans, 4 cups of water, bouillon cube, ham (cut into 4-6 chunks), bay leaves, thyme, oregano, salt, and dried red pepper to the pot.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil; reduce heat.
5. Cover and simmer until the beans are tender, about 1 to 1-1/2 hours.
6. Remove 1 cup of cooked beans from the stew and mash them in a bowl with a potato masher or fork.
7. Add the mashed beans back into the stew and stir to thicken.
8. Remove ham and dice it; discard bay leaves and dried red pepper (if used).
9. Add diced meat, green peppers, and rum (if desired) to the beans.
10. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
11. Serve beans over rice and top with sour cream if desired.
